heading: Inservice enrollment in a program of education
label: 3016 Inservice enrollment in a program of education
notes: References in TextSection 3011(a)(1)(A)(i), referred to in subsec. (a), was amended generally by , , , and, as so amended, does not contain a subcl. (I).Pub. L. 106–419, title I, § 103(a)(1)(A)114 Stat. 1825Nov. 1, 2000

Amendments1991—, renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 102–83, § 5(a)section 1416 of this titleSubsec. (a). , substituted “3011(c)(1)” for “1411(c)(1)”, “3012(d)(1)” for “1412(d)(1)”, “3012(b)(2)” for “1412(b)(2)”, and “3011(a)(1)(A)(i)(I) or 3012(a)(1)(A)(ii)” for “1411(a)(1)(A)(i)(I) or 1412(a)(1)(A)(ii)” in two places.Pub. L. 102–83, § 5(c)(1)Subsec. (b). , substituted “3012(a)(1)(B)(i)” for “1412(a)(1)(B)(i)” and “3012(a)(1)(B)(ii)” for “1412(a)(1)(B)(ii)” in two places.Pub. L. 102–83, § 5(c)(1)Subsec. (c). , substituted “3018(b)(3)(A)” for “1418(b)(3)(A)” and “3018(b)(2)” for “1418(b)(2)”.Pub. L. 102–83, § 5(c)(1)1988—Subsec. (c). added subsec. (c).Pub. L. 100–6891986— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section read as follows: “A member of the Armed Forces who has completed at least two years of service on active duty after , has continued on active duty or in the Selected Reserve without a break in service (except as described in ), and who but for section 1411(a)(1) or 1412(a)(1) of this title would be eligible for basic educational assistance may receive educational assistance under this chapter for enrollment in an approved program of education while continuing to perform the duty described in section 1411(a)(1) or 1412(a)(1) of this title.”Pub. L. 99–576section 1412(b)(2) of this titleJune 30, 1985
